content.text: Create a Python function that builds an Email message string from defined content, recipients, and attachments.
What specifically handles non-ASCII characters when setting the Content-Disposition header for an attachment?

If a `UnicodeEncodeError` occurs during ASCII encoding (usually in older Python versions), it catches the exception and manually encodes the filename using UTF-8 before adding the proper disposition headers.
This was very helpful to understand the email assembly logic!
